title: The welder at the empty docks
summary: Every Friday a retired Glasgow welder walks to the river to look at the empty docks.
body:
Every Friday, Iain walks to the river. He is seventy-four. He worked at the Glasgow shipyards for forty years. He was a welder. He retired in 2008.

The docks are empty now. The big cranes are gone. The sheds are gone. There are flats where the cranes were. Iain does not mind the flats, but he likes to see the water.

He walks the same path. From his small house to the bus stop, six minutes. From the bus stop to the river, twelve minutes. He sits on the same bench. He eats a sandwich. He looks at the water for one hour. Then he walks back.

Last Friday a young man sat next to him. The young man said, 'Excuse me, sir. Did you work here?'

Iain said, 'I built ships here for forty years. We made big ones. We made small ones. We made one for the navy that nobody saw again.'

The young man smiled. 'My grandfather worked here too. He was a painter. His name was Hugh Campbell.'

Iain thought for a long moment. He said, 'Hugh Campbell with the red beard. He painted the inside of the ships. He always sang while he painted. I remember him.'

The young man's eyes became wet. He said, 'Thank you.' He stood up and walked away.

Iain finished his sandwich. The water moved. He thought, the docks are empty, but the men are still here, in pieces, on benches. That is enough for a Friday.

annotations.meaningVi: không phiền — 'mind' + V-ing/N = bận tâm về
mắt rưng rưng — cách diễn tả nhẹ thay vì 'khóc'
từng mảnh / rời rạc — ở đây nghĩa bóng: ký ức rời rạc

grammarNotes.title: Past simple for finished work / habits long ago
grammarNotes.bodyVi: A2 dùng quá khứ đơn cho hành động đã hoàn thành trong quá khứ. Trong bài: 'He worked at the Glasgow shipyards for forty years.' 'He was a welder.' 'He retired in 2008.' 'I built ships here for forty years.' Động từ thường thêm '-ed' (worked, retired, walked); động từ bất quy tắc đổi dạng (build → built, sit → sat, stand → stood). Quá khứ đơn thường đi với mốc thời gian cụ thể: 'in 2008,' 'last Friday,' 'for forty years.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: A2 uses the past simple for completed actions and finished habits. From the passage: 'He worked at the Glasgow shipyards for forty years.' 'He was a welder.' 'He retired in 2008.' 'I built ships here for forty years.' Regular verbs add '-ed' (worked, retired, walked); irregular verbs change form (build → built, sit → sat, stand → stood). Past simple usually appears with a finished time marker: 'in 2008,' 'last Friday,' 'for forty years.'

translationVi:
Mỗi thứ Sáu, Iain đi bộ ra sông. Ông bảy mươi bốn tuổi. Ông làm việc ở xưởng đóng tàu Glasgow trong bốn mươi năm. Ông là thợ hàn. Ông nghỉ hưu năm 2008.

Bến tàu giờ trống không. Những cần cẩu lớn đã đi. Những nhà kho đã đi. Có những căn hộ nơi từng có cần cẩu. Iain không phiền chuyện các căn hộ, nhưng ông thích nhìn nước.

Ông đi cùng một lối. Từ căn nhà nhỏ đến trạm xe buýt, sáu phút. Từ trạm xe buýt đến sông, mười hai phút. Ông ngồi cùng một ghế băng. Ông ăn một chiếc bánh mì kẹp. Ông nhìn nước trong một giờ. Rồi ông đi về.

Thứ Sáu vừa rồi, một người trẻ ngồi xuống cạnh ông. Người trẻ nói: 'Xin lỗi bác. Bác từng làm ở đây ạ?'

Iain nói: 'Tôi đóng tàu ở đây bốn mươi năm. Chúng tôi đóng tàu to. Chúng tôi đóng tàu nhỏ. Có một chiếc cho hải quân mà sau đó không ai thấy lại.'

Người trẻ mỉm cười. 'Ông tôi cũng từng làm ở đây. Ông là thợ sơn. Tên ông là Hugh Campbell.'

Iain nghĩ một lát rất lâu. Ông nói: 'Hugh Campbell râu đỏ. Ông ấy sơn bên trong tàu. Ông ấy luôn hát khi sơn. Tôi nhớ ông ấy.'

Mắt người trẻ rưng rưng. Anh nói: 'Cảm ơn bác.' Anh đứng dậy và bước đi.

Iain ăn nốt bánh mì. Nước trôi. Ông nghĩ, bến tàu thì trống, nhưng các ông thợ vẫn còn đây, từng mảnh, trên những ghế băng. Thế là đủ cho một thứ Sáu.

questions.prompt: How often does Iain walk to the river?
questions.explanation: 'Every Friday, Iain walks to the river.'

questions.prompt: What job did he do at the shipyards?
What is at the docks now where the cranes used to be?
questions.explanation: 'There are flats where the cranes were.'

questions.prompt: How long does the walk from the bus stop to the river take?
questions.explanation: 'From the bus stop to the river, twelve minutes.'

options: The young man's grandfather, a painter at the shipyards.
questions.explanation: 'My grandfather worked here too. He was a painter.'

questions.prompt: What does Iain remember about Hugh Campbell?
options: He had a red beard, painted inside the ships, and sang while he painted.

questions.prompt: How does the young man react to Iain's memory?
options: His eyes become wet and he thanks Iain.
questions.explanation: 'The young man\'s eyes became wet. He said, "Thank you."'

questions.prompt: What does Iain mean by 'the men are still here, in pieces, on benches'?
options: The workers live on as scattered memories in people who sit by the river.
There are dead men buried under the benches.
Pieces of metal are on the benches.
The benches are made of old ship parts.
questions.explanation: It is a metaphor for memory being passed on through conversations.

options: A retired worker keeps the closed shipyards alive by carrying its people in memory and sharing them with strangers.
questions.explanation: The closing image of memory on benches.

questions.prompt: What does Iain do at the river each Friday?
options: Sits on the same bench, eats a sandwich, looks at the water for an hour.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Iain says the empty docks still hold the men 'in pieces, on benches.' Is there a place you visit not for what is there now, but for what it once held? What did sitting there give back to you?
